ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI for boondi; mapped to closest chickpea-based snack food class. Exact boondi entry absent from FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated boondi code; A0FJP (chickpea-based snack) used as closest defensible anchor. Sweetened deep-fried preparation noted.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for boondi or equivalent chickpea sweet snack as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical snack serving of boondi is approximately 30 g (about 2–3 tablespoons), providing roughly 140 kcal and 13 g of sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Boondi — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo entry for Boondi (F09010004). All core macros and secondary nutrients sourced from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated using USDA FoodData Central chickpea flour deep-fried snack analogue (USDA SR Legacy #19411) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; deep-fried chickpea flour snack analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for boondi found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 76 estimated from the closest analogous entry: deep-fried chickpea flour snack (sev/namkeen class, GI ~72–80) from Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Sweetened boondi has higher sugar content (43.8 g/100 g) which supports the upper range estimate of 76. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (76 × 21.9 g available carbohydrate per 30 g serving) / 100 = 16.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Extremely high sugar content (43.8 g/100 g) driving insulin-mediated sodium retention
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid boondi entirely; the 43.8 g/100 g sugar load triggers insulin-mediated sodium retention and fluid retention, worsening blood pressure control even though absolute sodium (33 mg/100 g) is low.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 43.8 g/100 g and high-GI deep-fried refined carbohydrate (total carbohydrate 57.4 g/100 g, GI ~76)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Boondi is contraindicated for Type 2 Diabetes management; sugar at 43.8 g/100 g far exceeds the 20 g/100 g glycaemic ceiling, and the high GI (~76) combined with deep-fried refined chickpea flour causes rapid, significant postprandial blood glucose spikes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (24.5 g/100 g) with estimated saturated fat ~5.5 g/100 g, trans fat ~0.2 g/100 g from deep-frying, and very high sugar (43.8 g/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should avoid boondi; the combination of deep-frying-derived saturated and trans fats, very high sugar promoting dyslipidaemia, and high caloric density (468 kcal/100 g) collectively elevate LDL cholesterol, triglycerides, and cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); sugar 43.8 g/100 g promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; trans fat 0.2 g/100 g
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Boondi should be avoided by patients with rheumatoid arthritis; deep-frying generates AGEs that amplify synovial inflammation, and the high sugar and trans fat content further promote systemic inflammatory pathways relevant to RA disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Boondi is a traditional Indian sweet snack made from chickpea flour (besan) batter that is forced through a perforated ladle into hot oil to form small spherical deep-fried pearls, which are then coated or soaked in sugar syrup. The result is a crunchy-to-chewy, intensely sweet confection with a characteristic golden-yellow colour and a faint legume flavour masked by sugar and cardamom. Boondi is widely consumed across the Indian subcontinent and among South Asian diaspora communities in Singapore and Southeast Asia, typically served as a festive snack, prasad (religious offering), or street food. Nutritionally, boondi is energy-dense at 468 kcal per 100 g, with very high sugar (43.8 g/100 g), moderate-to-high total fat (24.5 g/100 g) from deep-frying, and moderate carbohydrate (57.4 g/100 g). The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 76 (high), making it unsuitable for blood glucose management. Clinically, boondi is contraindicated for Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease due to its extreme sugar load, deep-frying-derived saturated and trans fats, and high caloric density. It is plant-based, gluten-free (chickpea flour base), and dairy-free, but should be treated as an occasional indulgence only.
common_user_queries: What is the GI of boondi?
How much sugar is in boondi?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Foodo (F09010004) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy chickpea deep-fried snack analogue. GI estimated from closest analogous entry in University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. All mandatory fields populated.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (5.5 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for deep-fried chickpea flour snack (closest analogue); HPB Foodo does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item. 2. Trans fat (0.2 g/100 g) is estimated based on typical deep-frying oil trans fat contribution for this preparation style; not directly reported by HPB. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific boondi preparation are not available in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 4. cholesterol_mg set to 0.0: boondi is a plant-based product with no animal-derived ingredients; cholesterol is confirmed zero by formulation. 5. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate based on deep-fried chickpea flour snack class from Atkinson et al. 2021; no direct GI measurement for sweetened boondi was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. food_group corrected from input 'Meat and alternatives' to 'Snacks' as boondi is a deep-fried sweet snack, not a meat alternative. 7. Chinese local names (邦迪甜点 / 邦迪甜點) are transliterations used in Singapore Chinese-language contexts; no established standard Chinese name exists for this Indian sweet. 8. Aliases self-check performed (iteration 3): every string value in aliases_by_language verified present verbatim in flat aliases array. english: [Boondi, Boondhi, Bundi, Sweet Boondi, Chickpea flour pearls, Besan boondi] — all present. chinese: [邦迪甜点, 邦迪甜點] — all present. malay: [Boondi] — present. tamil: [பூந்தி] — present. hindi: [बूंदी, मीठी बूंदी] — both present. romanized: [Boondi, Boondhi, Bundi, Meethi Boondi, Mithi Boondi] — all present. No missing entries detected.
